channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Blagojevich has new job working for ‘Bosnian Bear’ politician with ties to Putin
item.description: Former Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich will take on a new role representing the interests of a Serbian politician known as the “Bosnian Bear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE uncovers VA’s agreement to pay $380K per month for minor website modifications
item.description: DOGE discovered the Department of Veterans Affairs had a contract to have a company make minor modifications to the website for about $380K.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deadlocked St. Paul City Council sends interim council member decision to mayor
item.description: There were four finalists who interviewed for the seat. Three council members are in favor of one candidate, while the remaining three support another candidate.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna clashes with GOP House leaders, plans tele-town hall
item.description: The Pinellas County Republican is pushing to allow new parents the ability to vote by proxy, or remotely, for up to 12 weeks after having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SCOOP: White House rallies House GOP on Trump tariff plan in secret call
item.description: The White House held a call with House Republican aides rallying them around Trump's tariff plan, Fox News Digital was told.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Answering your questions about President Trump's vast new tariffs
item.description: After weeks of anticipation and speculation, President Donald Trump followed through on his reciprocal tariff threats by declaring on Wednesday a 10% baseline tax on imports from all countries and higher tariff rates on dozens of nations that run trade surpluses with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber And Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats demoralized by Trump get a boost from Wisconsin voters and Cory Booker's speech
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For a day, at least, Democrats across the country have a sense that their comeback against President Donald Trump may have begun.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These are the 'Dirty 15' countries Trump might target with Liberation Day tariffs
item.description: The White House has remained reticent regarding the specifics of the potential tariffs and the countries it plans to target. But these countries could be on the list.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Does President Trump really need to cut down Andrew Jackson's magnolia tree? Expert weighs in
item.description: Fox News Digital consulted an expert after President Trump revealed that a historic tree at the White House, originally planted by Andrew Jackson, is set to be cut down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitter House GOP divisions erupt after Johnson shuts down votes over Republican mutiny
item.description: House Republicans are still at odds after GOP leaders suffered an embarrassing defeat on the House floor by members of their own party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Army identifies 4th soldier who died in training accident in Lithuania
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army has released the identity of the fourth soldier who died in a training accident in Lithuania, a day after his body was recovered during a weeklong search that took hundreds of troops and other rescue workers from three nations.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces $100M deal with law firm tied to Doug Emhoff, Jan. 6 House panel
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced his administration struck a deal with a law firm with ties to former second gentleman Doug Emhoff and the House panel that investigated the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol as Trump targets major firms for retribution.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Who is Susan Crawford? Wisconsin Supreme Court winner has fought for union and abortion rights
item.description: Wisconsin Supreme Court winner Susan Crawford has served as a Dane County Circuit Court judge since 2018. Crawford won election that year and again in 2022 to the seat. The county is home to Wisconsin’s liberal capital, Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CCSD opposes school choice bill, while lawmakers say it empowers families
item.description: The Clark County School District is against a bill that would let parents enroll their children at any public school regardless of where they live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine wins special election for Florida's 6th Congressional District
item.description: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine won a special election Tuesday to represent Florida's 6th Congressional District, defeating Democratic challenger Josh Weil and squashing efforts by national Democrats who spent millions of dollars on the race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 20 hours and counting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker held the Senate floor with a marathon speech that lasted all night and into Tuesday afternoon in a feat of endurance to show Democrats’ objections to President Donald Trump’s sweeping actions.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republican Jimmy Patronis wins Matt Gaetz's Florida District 1 seat: DDHQ
item.description: Valimont raised almost $6.5 million compared to Patronis' $2.1 million, according to Federal Elections Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Green card holders, travelers caught in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: A lawful permanent resident who has lived in the U.S. for 50 years was detained because of a decades-old conviction amid tougher immigration enforcement at airports and border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: State Republicans say Sigal Chattah nominated for Nevada U.S. Attorney post
item.description: Nevada Republicans are saying Sigal Chattah has been nominated to serve as interim U.S. Attorney overseeing Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
